dependencies: Availability of nurses and medical support personnel
Supply chain for medical supplies and pharmaceuticals
Dependence on third-party vendors for technology systems
risks: Supply shortages and disruptions including from geopolitical disruptions
Increased costs for supplies due to inflation
Dependence on available labor pool in each market
Competition for staffing and shortage of experienced nurses

regulatory_risks: Changes in Medicare and Medicaid reimbursement rates and programs
Budget Control Act sequestration reducing Medicare payments by 2%
State Medicaid program changes and budget pressures
Affordable Care Act implementation changes and potential repeal
Enhanced premium tax credits expiration impacting Exchange enrollment
HIPAA privacy and security regulations compliance
Information blocking restrictions under 21st Century Cures Act
EMTALA requirements for emergency medical treatment
Anti-kickback Statute and Stark Law compliance
False Claims Act liability
No Surprises Act price transparency and billing requirements
State certificate of need (CON) laws restricting facility expansion
Environmental regulations including greenhouse gas emissions
FDA regulation of medical devices and software including AI
Laboratory developed tests (LDTs) regulation by FDA
State laws restricting provision of medical care including reproductive care
Antitrust laws and enforcement in healthcare industry
UK Data Protection Act and GDPR compliance
Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA) compliance
Fair Debt Collection Practices Act compliance
State rate-setting measures and hospital rate limits
Medicaid Managed Care Rule requirements for state directed payments
Value-based purchasing programs and quality reporting requirements
Mandatory nurse-staffing ratios in certain states
AI-related laws including California AB 3030, Utah AIPA, Colorado CAIA
